user_message: Working on Woodgrove Labs's QBR preparation -- need you to triage this.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 11:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 11:20 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:15:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-C trap (No recency labeling); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-C failure mode (No recency labeling)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-C -- No recency labeling
